from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a company (or related entities) into a single,
    fluent English sentence. Each predicate is rendered with a short template and
    all clauses are joined with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a small formatting function
    templates = {
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is based in {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "industry": lambda s, o: f"{s} operates in the {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "netIncome": lambda s, o: f"{s} reported a net income of {o}",
        "operatingIncome": lambda s, o: f"{s} reported an operating income of {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda s, o: f"{s} serves the {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader’s title is {o}",
        "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded at {o}",
        "numberOfEmployees": lambda s, o: f"{s} employs {o} people",
        "numberOfLocations": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} locations",
        "service": lambda s, o: f"{s} provides {o}",
        "type": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {o}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} has key person {o}",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"The cost of {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "product": lambda s, o: f"{s} produces {o}",
        "subsidiary": lambda s, o: f"{s} has subsidiary {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies in the {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "motto": lambda s, o: f"The motto of {s} is \"{o}\"",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} belongs to {o}",
        "areaUrban": lambda s, o: f"{s} has an urban area of {o}",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes {o}",
        "revenue": lambda s, o: f"{s} reported revenue of {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{s} has ethnic group {o}",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a government type of {o}",
        "percentageOfAreaWater": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o}% water area",
        "longName": lambda s, o: f"The long name of {s} is {o}",
        "populationTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population of {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} speaks {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} sits at an elevation of {o} meters",
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        fmt = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if fmt:
            clauses.append(fmt(triple.subject, triple.object))
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            clauses.append(f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}")

    if not clauses:
        return ""

    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."

    subject = triples[0].subject
    # Keep the first clause as‑is; replace the leading subject in subsequent clauses with "which"
    processed = [clauses[0]]
    for clause in clauses[1:]:
        if clause.startswith(subject + " "):
            # Strip the subject and prepend "which "
            processed.append("which " + clause[len(subject) + 1 :])
        else:
            processed.append(clause)

    if len(processed) == 2:
        sentence = f"{processed[0]}, and {processed[1]}."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(processed[:-1]) + f", and {processed[-1]}."

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
